location.office_time_required: primarily in-office; some teams offer WFH a day or two a week

level_signals: 2+ years of project/program management experience required
Compensation range $93,000–$160,000/year consistent with mid-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Relevant technical degree or experience in a software-related field (Systems Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, etc.)
2+ years of project/program management experience, preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment
must_haves.qualifier: preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated success managing complex software development projects for an enterprise software company or startup.
An ability to engage in technical discussion and invest in learning technical concepts.
Excellent judgment and composure in high-pressure situations.
A creative approach to project management centered around lightweight frameworks that enable rapid iteration, while operating in harmony with a larger development organization.
Building strong relationships with customer-facing teams and possessing high levels of empathy for our end-users' needs.
Meticulous attention to detail, including holding tightly to your team's vision and maintaining a high product quality bar.
Ability and willingness to obtain a US security clearance at the Secret designation or above

scope.surface: Palantir engineering teams across the full software development lifecycle; enterprise software products
core_responsibilities: Partner with Palantir software engineers, product managers, quality engineers, and business leadership to drive key product deliverables through the entire software development cycle.
Synthesize concrete technical goals from product vision, mapping global product strategy to granular team tasks and issues.
Keep broad and complete state of everything involving or related to your projects. Pre-empt and resolve any issues that may steer projects off-course.
Enhance cross-team collaboration. Read a room of technical experts to align them around shared objectives.
Work with customer-facing engineering teams on implementation, roll out, and support of your product.

green_flags: RSUs and sign-on bonus mentioned as additional compensation components
Generous benefits package including unlimited PTO and end-of-year paid leave
Clear articulation of core responsibilities and what the TPM role owns
Role is impactful across mission-critical domains (drug development, supply chain, child safety)
red_flags: Ability/willingness to obtain Secret-level US security clearance required — limits candidate pool significantly
No visa sponsorship information provided — unclear for international candidates
No specific cities listed for the role; location type inferred as hybrid from general company policy, not role-specific language
Salary range is wide ($93K–$160K), indicating potential ambiguity in level calibration
JD body describes hybrid/in-office culture but does not specify this role's exact city or office requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; assumed false from responsibilities)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from general company policy language; role-specific location not stated)

ambiguities: Location type: the JD body describes a general company preference for in-office work with some hybrid flexibility and rare remote exceptions, but does not specify whether this particular role is onsite, hybrid, or remote — treated as hybrid based on company-wide language.

Security clearance requirement appears under 'What We Value' (which typically signals preferred/soft items) but the language 'Ability and willingness to obtain' reads as a hard requirement — classified as hard severity.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
